code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Determine the primary subject (most frequent as subject)
    subject_counts = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_counts[t.subject] = subject_counts.get(t.subject, 0) + 1
    main_subject = max(subject_counts, key=subject_counts.get)

    # Mapping of predicates to readable phrase templates
    # `{}` will be replaced by the object value
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is situated {} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is situated {} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {} feet above sea level",
        "location": "is located in {}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {}",
        "country": "is located in {}",
        "language": "main language spoken is {}",
        "capital": "capital is {}",
        "demonym": "people are called {}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {}",
        "hubAirport": "has its hub airport at {}",
        "headquarter": "has its headquarters in {}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {} metres",
        "runwayLengthFeet": "has a runway length of {} feet",
        "runwayLengthMetre": "has a runway length of {} metres",
        "runwayName": "has a runway named {}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {}",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "its 1st runway surface is {}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "its 2nd runway surface is {}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "its 3rd runway surface is {}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "its 4th runway surface is {}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "its 5th runway surface is {}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "its 1st runway length is {} feet",
        "2ndRunwayLengthFeet": "its 2nd runway length is {} feet",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "its 3rd runway length is {} feet",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "its 4th runway length is {} feet",
        "5thRunwayLengthFeet": "its 5th runway length is {} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "its 1st runway length is {} metres",
        "2ndRunwayLengthMetre": "its 2nd runway length is {} metres",
        "3rdRunwayLengthMetre": "its 3rd runway length is {} metres",
        "4thRunwayLengthMetre": "its 4th runway length is {} metres",
        "5thRunwayLengthMetre": "its 5th runway length is {} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "its 1st runway number is {}",
        "2ndRunwayNumber": "its 2nd runway number is {}",
        "3rdRunwayNumber": "its 3rd runway number is {}",
        "4thRunwayNumber": "its 4th runway number is {}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "its 5th runway number is {}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has IATA code {}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {}",
        "nativeName": "is also known as {}",
        "owner": "is owned by {}",
        "leader": "is led by {}",
        "leaderParty": "belongs to the party {}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {}",
        "currency": "currency is {}",
        "regionServed": "serves the region {}",
        "class": "belongs to class {}",
        "division": "belongs to division {}",
        "order": "belongs to order {}",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction over {}",
        "countySeat": "county seat is {}",
        "chief": "chief is {}",
        "foundedBy": "founded by {}",
        "foundingYear": "founded in {}",
        "postalCode": "postal code {}",
        "areaCode": "area code {}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "ceremonial county {}",
    }

    # Helper to format numbers with commas and strip unnecessary decimals
    def _format_number(val: str) -> str:
        try:
            # Try to interpret as float/int
            num = float(val)
            if num.is_integer():
                return f"{int(num):,}"
            else:
                return f"{num:,}"
        except ValueError:
            return val

    # Helper to format a single triple
    def format_triple(t: Triple, include_subject: bool = False) -> str:
        """
        Return a natural‑language clause for a triple.
        If include_subject is True, the clause starts with the subject (used for the main entity).
        """
        # Helper to optionally prepend the subject
        def with_subj(text: str) -> str:
            return f"{t.subject} {text}" if include_subject else text

        # Specific phrasing for runway‑related predicates
        if t.predicate in {"1stRunwayLengthMetre", "1stRunwayLengthFeet"}:
            unit = "metres" if "Metre" in t.predicate else "feet"
            val = _format_number(t.object)
            return with_subj(f"has a first runway length of {val} {unit}")
        if t.predicate in {"runwayLength", "runwayLengthMetre", "runwayLengthFeet"}:
            unit = "metres" if "Metre" in t.predicate or t.predicate == "runwayLength" else "feet"
            val = _format_number(t.object)
            return with_subj(f"has a runway length of {val} {unit}")
        if t.predicate == "runwayName":
            return with_subj(f"has a runway named {t.object}")
        if t.predicate == "runwaySurfaceType":
            return with_subj(f"has runway surface {t.object}")
        if t.predicate in {"1stRunwaySurfaceType", "2ndRunwaySurfaceType", "3rdRunwaySurfaceType",
                           "4thRunwaySurfaceType", "5thRunwaySurfaceType"}:
            ordinal = {"1st":"first","2nd":"second","3rd":"third","4th":"fourth","5th":"fifth"}[t.predicate[:3]]
            return with_subj(f"has a {ordinal} runway surface {t.object}")

        # General predicates using the phrase map
        tmpl = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            obj = _format_number(t.object)
            # If the template already contains a verb, we may need the subject
            if include_subject:
                # Ensure the clause starts with the subject
                return with_subj(tmpl.format(obj))
            else:
                return tmpl.format(obj)

        # Fallback
        readable = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
        return with_subj(f"{readable} {t.object}")

    # Separate triples about the main subject from the rest
    main_clauses = []
    secondary_info: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            # Include the subject in the clause for natural flow
            clause = format_triple(t, include_subject=True)
            main_clauses.append(clause)
        else:
            clause = format_triple(t, include_subject=False)
            # Choose appropriate relative pronoun
            intro = "which"
            if t.predicate in {"language", "officialLanguage"}:
                intro = "where"
            secondary_info[t.subject] = (intro, clause)

    # Build the main sentence by joining clauses with commas and 'and'
    if not main_clauses:
        base_sentence = f"{main_subject}."
    elif len(main_clauses) == 1:
        base_sentence = main_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        base_sentence = ", ".join(main_clauses[:-1]) + " and " + main_clauses[-1] + "."

    # Append secondary information as relative clauses attached to the main subject
    if secondary_info:
        rel_clauses = []
        for subj, (intro, clause) in secondary_info.items():
            # Attach as a relative clause to the main subject
            rel_clauses.append(f"{intro} {clause}")
        # Combine all relative clauses
        base_sentence = base_sentence.rstrip(".") + " " + " ".join(rel_clauses) + "."

    # Clean up spacing
    full_sentence = " ".join(base_sentence.split())
    return full_sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
